description_long: This is a heavily armored tracked combat vehicle resembling a World War II-era German Tiger I tank. The vehicle features a large, angular turret mounted centrally on the hull, equipped with a long-barreled main cannon extending forward. The hull is rectangular with sloped armor on the front and sides, and the tracks are wide, running along the full length of the vehicle on both sides. The surface is textured and appears weathered, with visible hatches, periscopes, and other small details on the turret and hull. The vehicle is painted in a solid, worn gray color, with no visible camouflage or military markings. The overall design emphasizes protection, firepower, and mobility over rough terrain.
description: A heavily armored, tracked combat vehicle with a large turret and long cannon, finished in a worn gray color. The design is robust, with sloped armor and detailed surface features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tracks, the large turret, and the long cannon extending forward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sloped frontal armor, the width of the tracks, and the cannon pointing slightly to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the turret, tracks, and rear hull details, with the cannon pointing to the right.
description_view_3: Frontal perspective with the cannon aimed directly forward, showing the symmetry of the hull and turret.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One large cannon mounted on the central turret; no other weapon mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body coverage with thick, sloped steel armor plating on the hull and turret; no modular or reactive panels visible.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; solid worn gray paint scheme.
